title: iPhone Mirroring in macOS Sequoia and iOS 18: Everything You Need to Know
content:
Apple is expanding how its cross-platform Continuity features work in 2024, introducing an iPhone Mirroring option in macOS Sequoia and iOS 18. As the name suggests, ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring lets you control your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with your Mac.

Subscribe to the MacRumors YouTube channel for more videos.

This guide walks through all of the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring features and explains how it works.

iPhone Mirroring Basics


After upgrading to ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå and ‚ÄåmacOS Sequoia‚Äå, there's a new ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring app in the Dock, and that's how you start an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring session.



  1. From your Mac's Dock, click on the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring app.

  2. Click on Continue at the pop-up informational screen.

  3. Select the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå that you want to use if you have more than one ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå.

  4. Unlock your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with a passcode to allow the connection.

  5. Click on Get Started.

  6. Use Touch ID on the Mac to authenticate (alternatively, enter your password).


When you start up ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring for the first time, you can opt to have it require authentication with every connection or to allow a connection automatically. If you choose automatic authentication, you will not need to authenticate with ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå or a password to use ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring when your Mac is unlocked.


Otherwise, you will need to authenticate on your Mac each time you want to use ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring. Note that an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå needs to be locked while an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring session is in progress, so you can't use your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå while it's being mirrored to your Mac.

If your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå is not locked and you attempt to initiate ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring from your Mac, you will get a warning that the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå is in use.

There is no option for launching an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring session from an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå -- it needs to be done from a Mac. While the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring app icon should be in the Dock, if it's not there, you can get to it from the Launchpad or the Applications folder, because it is treated as a standard app.

iPhone Mirroring Interface


Your full ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå interface is shown as a dedicated app on the Mac, with your wallpaper and app icons all visible and accessible. You can click in to any of your apps and use it on the Mac just like you would on the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå.


You can get to your Home Screen by clicking on the bottom bar, and swap between pages with the trackpad or with a click and drag gesture using a mouse. There are menu options for getting to the ‚ÄåHome Screen‚Äå and for bringing up the app switching interface, but most control is done within the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring window.


iPhone Mirroring Requirements


To use ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring, you need a Mac that runs ‚ÄåmacOS Sequoia‚Äå and that has an Apple silicon chip (M1 or later) or a T2 security chip. Macs with T2 security chips are Intel-based and include the following machines:

  • 2018 and later MacBook Pro (13 and 15 inch)

  • 2018 and later MacBook Air

  • 2020 iMac

  • ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå Pro

  • 2018 Mac mini

  • 2019 Mac Pro


These Macs have Apple silicon chips:

  • 2020 and later ‚ÄåMac mini‚Äå

  • 2020 and later ‚ÄåMacBook Air‚Äå

  • 2021 and later ‚ÄåiMac‚Äå

  • 2021 and later MacBook Pro (14 and 16 inch)

  • 2020 13-inch MacBook Pro

  • 2022 and later Mac Studio

  • 2023 ‚ÄåMac Pro‚Äå


Any ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå that runs ‚ÄåiOS 18‚Äå works with ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring.

  • All iPhone 15 models

  • All iPhone 14 models

  • All iPhone 13 models

  • All ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 12 models

  • All ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 11 models

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XS and XS Max

  • ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå XR

  • iPhone SE (2nd and 3rd gen)


Your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå and Mac need to be signed into the same Apple ID, and two-factor authentication must be enabled for the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring feature to work. You will also need to turn on Bluetooth and Wi-Fi, and the two devices will need to be near each other.


During an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring session, your Mac and your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå need to be close to one another, essentially in the same room.

Privacy and Security


You need to be signed into the same ‚ÄåApple ID‚Äå on your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå and your Mac for ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring to work. While ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring is active, you do not need to have your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with you, and it does not need to be unlocked.


Your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå can remain tucked away in a pocket or a bag while you control it from your Mac, so no one can access it or see what you're doing with it.

Using a Mouse and Keyboard with iPhone


Your Mac's keyboard, trackpad, and mouse can be used with your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå, which is useful if you need to type something lengthy in an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå app.


Audio, Camera, and Microphone


If you play a video on your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå while ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring is enabled, the audio comes through your Mac. You can't turn on the Camera app or use the microphone, though.

iPhone Notifications on Mac


With ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring turned on, incoming ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå notifications show up right alongside your Mac notifications in the Notification Center at the upper right hand corner of the Mac's display.


‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå notifications are denoted with a small ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå icon, but they otherwise look like native Mac notifications.

You can click on a notification and open up the app on your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå, interacting with it directly from the Mac.

StandBy


While your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå is being mirrored to your Mac, it can be on a charger and in StandBy mode, displaying the time and other information.

Drag and Drop


Later this year, Apple plans to add ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring's best feature, file transfers. You will be able to transfer files, photos, and videos from your Mac to your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå and vice versa using drag and drop gestures.

Apple says that it will work just like dragging files from one Mac app to another.

While drag and drop has yet to be implemented, you can sort of work around this by using AirDrop to move photos and files from your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå to your Mac, initiating AirDrop from the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå without having to unlock it.

iPhone Mirroring Limitations


‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring will not work if the Mac is using AirPlay to stream music or video content to another device, or if Sidecar is enabled with an iPad. Continuity Camera cannot be enabled either, so if you're using your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå as a webcam for your Mac, you can't use ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring at the same time.

Attempting to watch movies and TV shows from some apps like Hulu and Netflix does not work, with the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring app showing just a blank black screen, likely due to DRM restrictions.

There is no option to resize the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring window, and you can't manually move it into landscape orientation. If an app or a game is displayed in landscape orientation, the window will shift into landscape.


You can delete apps when using ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå Mirroring by going to the Settings app, but apps cannot be deleted from the ‚ÄåHome Screen‚Äå. You also can't rearrange apps because there's no option to get into the ‚ÄåHome Screen‚Äå (or Lock Screen) editing interface.


Control Center and Notification Center are also inaccessible.
